rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. ROBYN KAUFMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Compliance With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs ROBERT CARTER, ROBYN KAUFMAN, BOBBIE CARTER, PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP, LTD.'s Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STATIONARY ENGINEERS LOCAL NO.39 INTERNATIONAL VS. ROBERTS & SONS BUILDERS, A DIVISION OF ROBERTS,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attoenys Fees And Costs To Defts As Prevailing Parties And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANTS DOUGLAS ROBERTS, MITCHELL ROBERTS, MATTHEW ROBERTS AND DANIEL ROBERTS'Motion For An Award Of Attoenys Fees And Costs To Defts As Prevailing Parties And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon is Granted in large. Defendants are awarded fees of $16,362.50 and costs of $4,510 for a total of $22,872. The fees request for this motion is reduced by $2000 since the motion is simple and required little or no research. All other fees and costs were reasonably incurred and defendants are entitled to recover them as prevailing parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents Set 2 And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 20, Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents Set 2 And Sanctions is Granted as to requests 15 and 16 and in camera review to be made of all documents responsive to requests 8, 10, 14 and 17. Neither State Fund, the Alameda County District Attorney nor the Department of Insurance has shown that disclosure of any information claimed by State Fund to be immune from discovery under Insurance Code 1877.4 would, if disclosed, jeopardize any ongoing investigation into the alleged wrongdoing of Dr. Cabayan or of any other person or entity. However, without review of the documents claimed to be privileged, the court cannot satisfy its obligation that any such investigation will not be jeopardized. Therefore, State Fund is required to provide all documents in its possession, custody and control that are responsive to requests 8, 10, 14 and 17 that it is withholding on the grounds of the section 1877.4 privilege to the court no later than June 1, 2012 for in camera review. A further hearing will occur on June 19, 2012 where the court will advise the parties of the results of the in camera review. The court will not be providing a court reporter for this discovery motion. If either side wishes a court reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SHINGLE SPRINGS TRIBAL GAMING AUTHORITY et al VS. HOEM & ASSOCIATES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Compel Response To Third Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT HOEM & ASSOCIATES, INC., A CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Response To Third Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions awarded against defendant Free Axez USA, LLC in the amount of $270 (assuming no hearing). The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and: (1) the Motion is not opposed; (2) Free Axez has not submitted any response or proposed response to the Request for Production. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT WINSTON LUM MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Granted. The void default is vacated based on equity and Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d). The default never should have been entered because (1) plaintiff never had leave to make new allegations against Mr. Lum and (2) Mr. Lum's answer to the original complaint was sufficient to contest the allegations of the amended complaint because the amended complaint was not substantively different with regard to Mr. Lum. Mr. Lum has leave to file an amended answer if he wishes to do so by June 1, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER GIAKOVMIS MA VS. VLADIMIR RUDAKOV, COUNTY ADULT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reconsider "Order Granting Defts Second Motion To Quash"
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF HEATHER GIAKOVMIS Notice Of Motion To Reconsider "Order Granting Defts Second Motion To Quash" IS Denied. The motion is untimely under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, which requires that motions for reconsideration be filed within ten days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order. Plaintiff must re-serve Defendant with the summons and complaint and file her proof of service with the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: DIMITRIOS STAVRAKIS VS. LILLIAN BAU LIN FAN AKA LILLIAN CHOU, AKA LILLIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate As Void The Order Dated Jan-11-12 Entered By The Judge Pro Tempore
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT DAVY CHOU, LILLIAN FAN Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate As Void The Order Dated Jan-11-12 Entered By The Judge Pro Tempore Denied on the grounds asserted in the motion, yet the motion is meritorious if made pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473 and, if defendants wish to proceed under that section, the motion is granted. Temporary Judge Mark Schreiber did not act without jurisdiction in issuing the order sought to be vacated. After sufficient notice to them that the motion would be heard by Temporary Judge Schreiber, defendants' failure to appear at the hearing was properly deemed consent to the motion being heard by Temporary Judge Schreiber. Nonetheless, it is undisputed that defendants' non-appearance was due solely to a calendaring error, which is a sufficient basis for relief under section 473. If defendants wish to proceed under that section, the order issued by Temporary Judge Schreiber will be vacated and plaintiffs' motion for discovery sanctions will be heard on June 14, 2012 at 9:30am before Judge Kahn provided that defendants deliver courtesy copies of all papers filed by the parties on the motion to department 302 no later than 4pm on May 29 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. If defendants wish to obtain relief per section 473, then, if plaintiffs wish to seek monetary sanctions per that section, plaintiffs may file a motion no later than June 1 seeking such sanctions, defendants may file opposition papers no later than June 8, and the hearing will be on June 14 without the filing of any reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST CHIN VS. MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 23, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANTS MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INCORPORATED AND EVGUENI PERELMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Granted as to paragraphs 6 and 9 of the prayer for relief and denied as to all other allegations sought to be stricken. Plaintiff concedes that the Probate Code sections are inapplicable. The requests for punitive damages and attorney's fees, and the fourth cause of action are adequately pled. These claims are properly pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
